agreement: A legally binding arrangement between two or more parties.
contract: A formal written or spoken agreement that is enforceable by law.
tenant: A person who rents land or property from a landlord.
landlord: A person who rents out property to a tenant.
rent: A payment made periodically for the use of property.
notice: A formal legal warning or announcement.
terminate: To legally end an agreement or contract.
breach: Failure to comply with the terms of a legal agreement.
court: A body that resolves legal disputes and interprets the law.
property: Land or buildings owned by a person or organization.
jurisdiction: The authority of a court to hear and decide cases.
